description_long: This object consists of a group of apartment buildings of varying sizes and heights, arranged in a row. The buildings feature flat roofs, rectangular forms, and a mix of window arrangements, with some facades displaying repetitive, grid-like window patterns. The exteriors are primarily finished in shades of gray and beige, with some buildings showing subtle color variations and weathering effects. The structures appear to be constructed from concrete and glass, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century urban residential architecture. Some buildings have visible balconies, while others have plain facades. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian, functional design commonly found in dense urban neighborhoods.
description: A collection of mid-rise apartment buildings with flat roofs, grid-like window patterns, and muted gray exteriors, typical of urban residential blocks.
description_view_0: A frontal view showing the full lineup of apartment buildings, highlighting their varying heights and facade details.
description_view_1: A side view emphasizing the depth and length of the buildings, with rooftops and building footprints clearly visible.
description_view_2: A rear or alternate frontal view, displaying the opposite facades and the arrangement of the buildings in a row.
description_view_3: A second side view, similar to view 1 but from the opposite direction, showing the backs and sides of the buildings.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in regular, evenly spaced grids across the facades, with some buildings featuring grouped or stacked window patterns for symmetry.
